product_overview: Archon is an AI-powered platform that generates structured, navigable documentation from GitHub codebases. Users create documentation projects by pointing Archon at public HTTPS repositories, monitor generation progress, and publish browsable documentation with search-friendly pages and diagrams.
core_goals: Automatically produce comprehensive, navigable documentation from one or more public GitHub repositories
Let users create and configure documentation projects with repository and branch selection and optional AI instructions
Provide real-time visibility into documentation generation progress and control (cancel/regenerate)
Enable users to publish, view, and manage generated documentation with hierarchical navigation and rendered markdown
Allow owners to edit documentation metadata, repository associations, and visibility (public/private)

features.name: Landing Page
features.description: Introduction and entry point that explains the product, highlights capabilities, and directs users to get started.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> See hero section with product description -> View feature cards summarizing capabilities -> Read how-it-works 3-step process -> Use call-to-action to go to authentication

features.name: User Authentication (email/password)
features.description: Sign in or sign up using email and password; session-based access to the dashboard. (OAuth flows are available in the app but are excluded from automated flows.)
user_flows: Navigate to /auth -> Switch to Sign In tab -> Fill in email -> Fill in password -> Submit sign-in form -> Redirect to /app -> See documentation dashboard

Navigate to /auth -> Switch to Sign Up tab -> Fill in email with a whitelisted domain -> Fill in password meeting length requirement -> Submit sign-up form -> Redirect to /app -> See documentation dashboard

Navigate to /auth -> Switch to Sign Up tab -> Fill in email with a non-whitelisted domain -> Fill in password -> Submit sign-up form -> See validation error message indicating email domain is not allowed
Navigate to /auth -> Switch to Sign Up tab -> Fill in email -> Fill in password shorter than required -> Submit sign-up form -> See validation error message for password length

features.name: Documentation Dashboard
features.description: Central hub showing all user documentation projects, status indicators, visibility, and quick actions to create or manage projects.
user_flows: Navigate to /app -> View grid of documentation project cards -> See project status badge for each project (generating/failed/completed) -> Open project details for a completed project -> See published status and visibility
Navigate to /app -> See empty state with onboarding guidance when no projects exist -> Start creating a new documentation project -> Redirect to /app/new
Navigate to /app -> View a project with failed generation status -> Retry generation -> See project status update to pending/running

features.name: Create Documentation (multi-step wizard)
features.description: Guided five-step wizard to name a documentation, choose its type, add repositories, select branches, and provide optional AI instructions, then start generation.
user_flows: Navigate to /app/new -> Enter documentation name -> Select documentation type (onboarding/developer/user) -> Add one or more valid HTTPS repository URLs -> Select branch for each repository -> Enter optional custom AI instructions -> Submit form to create documentation and enqueue generation -> Redirect to generation status page
Navigate to /app/new -> Attempt to submit without entering a documentation name -> See validation error indicating name is required
Navigate to /app/new -> Add a repository URL that is not HTTPS -> Attempt to proceed -> See validation error message indicating only HTTPS repository URLs are supported

features.name: Generation Status Tracking
features.description: Real-time polling view that shows progress of AI-driven documentation generation, with options to cancel or regenerate.
user_flows: After submitting a new documentation project -> Redirect to generation status page -> See generation progress status and spinner updating via polling -> Wait until generation completes -> See success message and option to open generated documentation
After submitting a new documentation project -> Redirect to generation status page -> See running generation status -> Cancel generation -> See cancelled status
On generation failure -> See failure message with error details -> Choose to regenerate with optional new instructions -> See status change to pending/running

features.name: Edit Documentation
features.description: Manage documentation metadata, repositories and branches, visibility, and lifecycle actions such as regenerate or delete.
user_flows: Navigate to /app -> Open documentation settings for a project -> Edit documentation name and description -> Save changes -> See updated metadata reflected in the dashboard
Navigate to /app -> Open documentation settings -> Add or remove repository HTTPS URLs and select branches -> Save changes -> See updated repository list for the documentation
Navigate to /app -> Open documentation settings -> Toggle visibility from private to public -> Save changes -> See visibility indicator updated
Navigate to /app -> Open documentation settings -> Choose Delete documentation -> Confirm deletion -> See documentation removed from dashboard

features.name: View Documentation
features.description: Browse published documentation pages with rendered markdown, syntax highlighting, diagrams, hierarchical navigation, and owner actions when applicable.
user_flows: Open a published documentation URL -> See sidebar with hierarchical page list and groups -> Select a page from the sidebar -> Read rendered markdown content with syntax highlighting and rendered mermaid diagrams -> Use previous/next links to navigate sequential pages
Open a published documentation URL as the owner -> See an Edit action available -> Start editing documentation settings or trigger regeneration
Open a published documentation URL -> Use Ask AI action to send the current page context to an external AI tool (ChatGPT/Claude/Cursor) via a pre-filled prompt

routes.description: Marketing landing page with hero section, feature cards, and how-it-works section
Sign in and sign up page with email/password and GitHub OAuth
Dashboard showing user documentation projects in a grid with status indicators
Multi-step wizard to create new documentation with name, type, repos, branches, and instructions
Real-time documentation generation status with polling, cancel, and regenerate options
Edit documentation settings, manage repositories, toggle visibility, regenerate or delete
Documentation root that redirects to first page
Documentation page viewer with markdown rendering, syntax highlighting, mermaid diagrams, and sidebar navigation

features.name: User Authentication
features.description: Sign in and sign up with email/password or GitHub OAuth, with session management
user_interactions: Switch between Sign In and Sign Up tabs
Fill email input field
Fill password input field
Click Sign In or Sign Up button
Click GitHub OAuth button
See validation error messages for invalid inputs
Redirect to /app dashboard on successful authentication

features.name: Documentation Dashboard
features.description: View all user documentation projects with status, visibility, and quick actions
user_interactions: View grid of documentation project cards
See project status badge (generating, failed, completed)
Click View button to open generated documentation
Click Refresh to retry failed generation
Click New Documentation button to start creation wizard
See empty state with onboarding guidance when no projects exist

features.name: Create Documentation
features.description: Multi-step wizard to create new documentation project with repository links
user_interactions: Enter documentation name in step 1
Select documentation type (onboarding, developer, user) in step 2
Add repository HTTPS URLs in step 3
Select branch for each repository in step 4
Enter custom AI instructions in step 5
Navigate between wizard steps using Next and Back buttons
Submit to start documentation generation

features.name: Generation Status
features.description: Monitor documentation generation progress with real-time polling
user_interactions: View generation progress status
Click Cancel to stop generation
Click Regenerate on failure or completion
Navigate to documentation when generation completes

features.name: Edit Documentation
features.description: Edit documentation settings, manage repositories, and control visibility
user_interactions: Edit documentation name and description
Add or remove repository URLs
Select branches for repositories
Toggle public/private visibility
Click Regenerate to update documentation
Click Delete to remove documentation

features.name: View Documentation
features.description: Browse generated documentation with markdown rendering and navigation
user_interactions: Read markdown content with syntax highlighting
View mermaid diagrams
Navigate pages using sidebar
Click Previous/Next page links
Click Ask AI to interact with AI platforms
Click Edit to modify documentation (if owner)

features.name: Landing Page
features.description: Marketing landing page with product overview and call-to-action
user_interactions: View hero section with product description
View feature cards highlighting platform capabilities
Read how-it-works section with 3-step process
Click call-to-action button to get started

known_limitations.issue: Only public HTTPS repositories supported, no SSH or private repos
known_limitations.impact: Users cannot add private repositories or use SSH URLs

known_limitations.issue: Email domain restriction limits sign-up to specific domains
known_limitations.impact: Users with non-whitelisted email domains cannot sign up

known_limitations.issue: No collaborative editing or version history for documentation
known_limitations.impact: Only single-user editing, no change tracking

known_limitations.issue: Dev server runs single-threaded and may be unstable under concurrent test load
known_limitations.impact: Test stability may be affected when running in development mode
